normalized_title: Position Statement: Planning for Tourism and Short-term Rental Accommodation
scope_summary: WAPC position statement guiding the location and management of tourism land uses and short-term rental accommodation (hosted and unhosted) through the WA planning framework. It applies to all tourism and short-term rental accommodation proposals across Western Australia and directs how local planning strategies, schemes, policies, structure plans and development/subdivision decisions should treat tourism zones, caravan parks and short-term rentals. Within the Perth Metropolitan Area it sets a single rule requiring development approval for unhosted short-term rental accommodation outside tourism zones exceeding 90 nights in a 12-month period.

key_numeric_standards.topic: unhosted short-term rental exemption threshold (Perth Metropolitan Area)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: unhosted short-term rental accommodation of a dwelling; letting for 90 nights or less requires no development approval

key_numeric_standards.topic: development approval trigger for unhosted short-term rental (Perth Metropolitan Area)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: unhosted short-term rental accommodation outside tourism zones; zoning tables must make it a D or A use requiring approval
